beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check emails and news
beats.reasoning: Cyrus starts his Monday morning at home with full energy; 35 min covers shower, breakfast, and mental prep for the week.
Early commute to Renton workplace avoids rush; 35 min accounts for light Monday traffic and crossing the I-90 corridor.

beats.activity: Tax season accounting work; emails, client returns, quarterly filings
beats.reasoning: Early June is tail end of tax season; Cyrus works steadily through the morning, stopping before the 11am FIFA match he's aware of.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle for lunch and the USA vs Wales match
beats.reasoning: Cyrus heads back to Seattle to catch the 11am match (already started, but he can catch the second half); 35 min drive from Renton, arrives by noon.

beats.activity: Lunch and watch USA vs Wales FIFA match at a bar
beats.reasoning: Community-minded Cyrus enjoys the energy of a sports bar on a sunny Monday; he catches the second half of the match with a burger and beer.
Post-lunch stroll through downtown; Cyrus can't resist browsing a bookstore, especially on a perfect June afternoon; 30 min walk/transit from Capitol Hill.

beats.activity: Browse books, pick up a hiking memoir
beats.reasoning: Cyrus spends over an hour indulging his bookstore habit, selecting titles on trail running and adventure for his off-season reading.

beats.activity: Head to Green Lake for an evening trail run
beats.reasoning: Sunny 74F weather is perfect for outdoor exercise; 40 min transit/walk from Pioneer Square to Green Lake.

beats.activity: Trail run around Green Lake loop
beats.reasoning: Cyrus takes advantage of the beautiful weather for a 75-minute evening run; his perfectionist nature means consistent training.
40 min drive from Green Lake back to home; allows time to cool down and transition to evening.

beats.activity: Dinner prep and eat at home; read new hiking book
beats.reasoning: Post-run recovery meal; Cyrus settles in with his new book purchase to unwind and reflect on the day.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: read, light tidying, prepare for Tuesday
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home; Cyrus enjoys his new book, does light housekeeping, and mentally prepares for the work week ahead.
7.5 hours of sleep; Cyrus gets solid rest after an active day, ready for Tuesday morning.

diary:
Woke up early with the coffee maker—tax season brain still running even though June's here. Got through the morning filing work in Renton, then made an impulsive decision to head back into the city for the World Cup match. Kremwerk was packed with folks who had the same idea. I'm not usually one for crowded bars, but there was something about that energy, everyone leaning toward the screens. The USA played decent.

After, I wandered over to Elliott Bay and lost an hour browsing. Picked up a memoir about the Cascades that caught my eye. Then drove out to Green Lake for a proper run—needed it after sitting all day. The trail was perfect in that afternoon light, almost empty. Finished the evening at home with the new book and some quiet. My legs feel good and tired.
thought_process: I felt the pull of the season changing today—June hitting different. Tax work is wrapping, which loosens something in me. The World Cup match was spontaneous; normally I'd skip it, but the weather was too nice and the bar energy seemed like a counterweight to the morning's spreadsheets. I needed the break more than I admitted. The bookstore was a natural detour, and that hiking memoir felt like permission to start thinking about real trails again instead of just the treadmill grind. The run around Green Lake reset everything. By evening I was settled, ready for the week ahead, but already imagining longer hikes.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
